title: marshal: concurrent marshal.dumps() of a shared list races on the list's ob_item[] array (w_complex_object)
description: w_complex_object serializes a list with PyList_GET_ITEM(v, i) (a plain, non-atomic read of ob_item[]) and takes no critical section on the list, unlike the set branch of the same function. marshal.dumps(shared_list) on one thread races with list.append() on another thread, whose atomic ob_item[] store (element slot on the fast path, or ob_item base pointer on the resize path) collides with the marshaller's plain read. marshal.dumps() looks read-only, so a shared list is not safe to marshal concurrently with mutation.
sites: Python/marshal.c w_complex_object:605 (read PyList_GET_ITEM(v, i) -> ob_item[i], non-atomic, no critical section)
Objects/listobject.c list_resize:165 / Include/internal/pycore_list.h _PyList_AppendTakeRef:46 (atomic release store into ob_item[] by list.append on the other thread)
race_kind: unsynchronized read of a shared mutable container (marshal reader not converted to critical-section/atomic; list.append writer is atomic)
shared_state: PyListObject.ob_item[] (element slots on the fast-path append; the ob_item base pointer on the resize/realloc path) of a list shared across threads
severity: medium (element-slot variant is value-benign/crash-free; the list_resize variant is a use-after-free hazard when the old ob_item buffer is freed while the marshaller reads it, mitigated but not guaranteed by QSBR-deferred free)
repro_reliability: deterministic (exit 66, races on the first round)
suggested_fix: wrap the list (and dict, defensively tuple) serialization loop in w_complex_object with Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(v)/Py_END_CRITICAL_SECTION(), mirroring the set/frozenset branch (marshal.c:655) which already does this; no-op on the default GIL build
notes: Vehicle shared a list [1,2,3] across 4 workers; op (b) called marshal.dumps(shared_list) while op (f) did shared_list.append(_i). Reader side uses raw PyList_GET_ITEM (Include/cpython/listobject.h:40) with no lock; writer side (list.append) uses _Py_atomic_store_ptr_release. Same 'writer hardened for FT, reader still plain' class as other fixed CPython FT read-path races. The set branch is the only container branch in w_complex_object currently protected by a critical section; list/tuple/dict should be audited together.
disposition: real bug (shared-list non-atomic reader face) -- RULED a bug by Yhg1s 2026-07-15
assessment: Real free-threading bug: marshal.dumps(shared_list) reads ob_item[] via plain PyList_GET_ITEM in w_complex_object while another thread appends -- a reader face of the shared-builtin read-while-mutate class that Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s) ruled a bug on 2026-07-15. The list_resize variant is additionally a UAF hazard (old ob_item freed under the marshaller's index). Fix: take Py_BEGIN_CRITICAL_SECTION(v) around the list/dict walk (the set branch of the same w_complex_object already does).
ruling: Thomas Wouters (Yhg1s, CPython RM), 2026-07-15: shared-builtin concurrent-access class 'is a bug'
related: TSAN-0013 (the Py_SIZE/bytes_join reader faces of the same class)
fleet02_notes: fleet-02 variant: the list_resize write-side vs the marshal reader (same race)
